title: anthosachne kingiana subsp. multiflora
intro: Anthosachne kingiana subsp. multiflora, also known as the many-flowered tussock grass, is a type of flowering grass native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Anthosachne kingiana subsp. multiflora include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
